name: presentation-feature-video-ads
slide_count_policy: prefer_more_shorter_scenes; for normal-length content, default to 20+ micro-scenes; use the smallest deck only after splitting rich content into one-focus scenes; favor visual beats over copy-heavy slides
scene_density_policy: split scenes when they contain multiple major claims; avoid text-heavy composite scenes
scene_archetype_policy: use centered statement, split copy/product, full-bleed product showcase, proof-grid/stat-wall, transition, and CTA lockup scenes; do not let split layouts dominate the whole deck
layout_variance_policy: alternate dominant scene archetypes and spatial compositions so the same layout does not repeat more than twice in a row
single_focus_policy: each scene should keep one focal composition and one visible UI state
chapter_pacing_policy: use 2-3 scenes for major feature families when the product surface evolves across states
simulation_pattern_policy: favor split text/sim layouts, modern product mockups, counters, timelines, chips, staged state changes, and a mix of title-only, text+mockup, UI-only, and transition scenes
copy_budget_policy: default to one headline or short label plus at most one short support line; allow visual-only scenes; split any scene that needs more than two short lines
motion_density_policy: every scene should include at least one motion event such as text reveal, card entrance, counter tick, state swap, camera drift, a small UI interaction, or a fade-based presence transition
text_layer_motion_policy: text layers should fade in and fade out cleanly; do not pop text in over other text or overlay it onto cramped surfaces
motion_family_variance_policy: vary the dominant motion family from scene to scene; rotate word reveals, fades, counters, state swaps, card entrances, and camera drift
scene_mix_policy: mix text-light, visual-only, proof, and transition scenes; do not build text-heavy multi-card slides
recheck_policy: run a final repair pass for overlap, duplicate dominant layouts, and slide-like scenes before delivery
overlay_safety_policy: reject overlapping text layers, clipped controls, or competing content clusters; split the scene instead
visual_discovery_policy: use generated preview cards from templates/*/preview.md before opening a design doc
anti_ai_slop_policy: curated distinct styles only; avoid generic template aesthetics and purple-gradient-on-white defaults
background_layer_policy: every scene must have a static full-viewport background layer rendered outside the scaled stage with no self-animation
background_static_policy: backgrounds must fill the viewport using cover sizing and must not use transforms or continuous animation; only opacity transitions are allowed
mobile_9_16_policy: after composing at 16:9, recheck every scene at 9:16; stack layouts, cap headline at 3rem subline at 1.25rem body at 1rem, add 40px safe-zone padding, and remove overflow or clipped text
accessibility_policy: semantic markup, keyboard controls, aria labels, and contrast-safe text

story_arc.name: concept_reframe
story_arc.purpose: reframe the category or value proposition

story_arc.name: feature_chapters
story_arc.purpose: polished simulation-card chapters sized to the source content

story_arc.name: proof_and_fit
story_arc.purpose: audience, comparison, or evidence scenes when supported

surface_traits: fixed 16:9 primary canvas with 9:16 mobile-safe variant
static full-viewport background layer rendered outside the scaled stage

fallback_rules.bundled_reference_authority: use the bundled style guide, scene grammar, and example briefs as the source of truth
fallback_rules.content_sparse: compress to fewer strong claims rather than invent new sections; otherwise split content into more, shorter scenes

about_us.expected_result: About Us Presentation in the bundled PresentationFeature house style, using split scenes and UI-heavy proof moments

pricing.expected_result: content-sized pricing presentation in the bundled house style, with short scenes and visible comparison UI
